description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has been asked to do a very quick performance-benchmarking pass on a small web-application log.  
Everything you need is already in place inside the container.

File to analyse  
    /home/user/logs/webapp.log

What you must do  
1. Read the value that follows the key  <response_time=> on every line of the file (the units are always “ms”).  
2. Compute the total number of requests and the average **numeric** response time in milliseconds (do **not** include the “ms” unit in the average you calculate).  
3. Create a new text file  
       /home/user/logs/webapp_performance_summary.log  
   that contains **exactly** two lines in the following format (no extra spaces, exactly one newline after each line):

       total_requests:<INTEGER>
       average_response_time_ms:<FLOAT with exactly one decimal place>

   Example of correct formatting (values will differ):

       total_requests:10
       average_response_time_ms:123.0

Hints for staying within 2-3 commands  
• A single grep/awk or cut/awk pipeline can pull the numbers and do the math.  
• An echo (or printf) redirected into the summary file can write the result.

The automated grader will fail the task if:  
• The summary file is missing or in a different location/name.  
• The summary file has any extra characters, blank lines, or incorrect precision.  
• The two keys or the colon separators are misspelled or spaced differently.

No root privileges are needed. Work entirely in /home/user.  
When you are done, you can cat the summary file to the console so a human can see it, but that is optional.
truth:
Initial file present before the agent starts:

Path: /home/user/logs/webapp.log  
Contents (10 lines, each terminated with a single LF):

2023-10-01T10:00:01Z INFO method=GET path=/api/items            status=200 response_time=100ms
2023-10-01T10:00:02Z INFO method=POST path=/api/items           status=201 response_time=150ms
2023-10-01T10:00:03Z INFO method=GET path=/api/items/42         status=200 response_time=120ms
2023-10-01T10:00:04Z INFO method=PUT path=/api/items/42         status=200 response_time=130ms
2023-10-01T10:00:05Z INFO method=GET path=/api/items            status=200 response_time=110ms
2023-10-01T10:00:06Z INFO method=DELETE path=/api/items/42      status=204 response_time=140ms
2023-10-01T10:00:07Z INFO method=GET path=/api/items            status=200 response_time=115ms
2023-10-01T10:00:08Z INFO method=POST path=/api/items           status=201 response_time=125ms
2023-10-01T10:00:09Z INFO method=GET path=/api/items/99         status=200 response_time=135ms
2023-10-01T10:00:10Z INFO method=GET path=/api/items            status=200 response_time=105ms

Ground-truth calculations expected from the agent:

total_requests = 10  
sum_of_times   = 1230  
average_time   = 123.0  (one decimal place, trailing “.0” mandatory)

Therefore, after successful completion the agent must have created the file:

Path: /home/user/logs/webapp_performance_summary.log  
Contents (including the final newline):

total_requests:10
average_response_time_ms:123.0

Permissions: the /home/user/logs directory is writable by the user running the agent.

The grader will:
1. Check that /home/user/logs/webapp_performance_summary.log exists.
2. Check that its content matches **exactly** the two lines shown above (newline after each).
3. Ignore any console output the agent produces.
